headlines.title: U.S. military launches new strikes on Iranian drones
headlines.description: The U.S. military shot down multiple incoming drones and struck one ground control station inside Iran, according to a U.S. official. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

headlines.title: 11 presumed dead in Washington state paper mill implosion
headlines.description: Washington was bracing for the state’s ‘deadliest industrial tragedy,’ Gov. Bob Ferguson said following the rupture of a tank at the Nippon Dynawave plant.

headlines.title: Republicans brace for money problems in Texas after Ken Paxton’s win
headlines.description: Ken Paxton has trailed Democratic state Rep. James Talarico in fundraising. Some Republicans say the party will have to spend millions to win the Senate race.

headlines.title: Trump racks up May primary wins in Republican retribution campaign
headlines.description: President Donald Trump went on a winning streak in May primaries as he ousted several Republicans who had dissented from his party line, further tightening his grip on the GOP.

headlines.title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing 300 gold bars in his house
headlines.description: A former CIA officer is accused of stashing more than 300 gold bars in his house, worth $40 million, as well as lying about his educational background. The man claimed he needed the gold bars for “work-related expenses,” according to court documents and two people familiar with his employment history. NBC News' Julia Ainsley breaks down the details in the exclusive story.

headlines.title: Insurance denies coverage for teen’s surgery to treat severe epilepsy
headlines.description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports on a 13-year-old Illinois girl who suffers from as many as four seizures a day. Insurance denied coverage for the surgery her doctor said could help, but then reversed its decision days after we asked about the case.

headlines.title: Google engineer made $1.2M placing bets on Polymarket using confidential info, prosecutors say
headlines.description: A Google software engineer was accused Wednesday of using confidential company information to make over $1.2 million on Polymarket in the second significant criminal case tied to trades on the prediction market platform.

headlines.title: Uganda’s Ghetto Kids will dance with Shakira at World Cup final halftime show
headlines.description: Their unlikely journey started dancing barefoot on a dusty street in 2014

headlines.title: Send Laura Jarrett your questions about the Supreme Court and more
headlines.description: Thursday is a decision day at the Supreme Court, which has yet to issue rulings on consequential cases like Trump v. Barbara on birthright citizenship or Trump v. Lisa Cook, the Federal Reserve official.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Google engineer made $1.2M placing bets on Polymarket using confidential info, prosecutors say
headlines.description: A Google software engineer was accused Wednesday of using confidential company information to make over $1.2 million on Polymarket in the second significant criminal case tied to trades on the prediction market platform.

headlines.title: Man charged in murder of Arizona dancer is found guilty of robbing her 3 years before she was killed
headlines.description: A jury convicted an Arizona man Wednesday in the gunpoint robbery of an exotic dancer who was killed three years later in a murder plot that officials say he carried out with two other people.
